red_baseline.reason: T017 froze the official-SDK real-process handshake, tool projection, error, framing, concurrency, lifecycle-independence, and Plugin-cwd scope contract.
red_baseline.summary: All six real-wire scenarios fail at connection close because src/mcp/stdio-server.js is absent.

validation.summary: 6/6 official-SDK real-process protocol, concurrency, adapter restart, output isolation, and Plugin-cwd scope scenarios passed.
49 passed, 1 existing POSIX-only skip, and 0 failures across shared process capture, lock, journal, task, and CLI contracts.
Syntax check passed for 91 JavaScript files.
The npm payload contains launchdeck-mcp plus the stdio server, diagnostics, tool projection, schemas, and shared Kernel/runtime sources.
Full serialized suite: 247 total, 246 passed, 1 existing platform skip, 0 failures.

review.evidence: Bounded review confirmed one official SDK stdio transport, registry/schema-derived tools, explicit registered-project resolution, shared Kernel lifecycle/journal/clean authorities, bounded diagnostic stderr and foreground output, protocol-only stdout, and no adapter-owned managed-task lifetime.

obligation_evidence.evidence: All 18 MCP tools call the same Application Kernel and existing registry, run, ownership, process, event, clean, and operation-journal authorities used by CLI.
The projection exposes only the approved low-risk catalog; medium task execution and nested force inputs refuse before effects.
Runtime provenance identifies source stdio/package-mcp and current host only; no Plugin, bundle, update, or other-OS readiness is claimed.
Every public MCP mutation reaches Application Kernel policy and ownership resolution before the shared effect handler.
MCP annotations are derived descriptive hints; Kernel policy independently refuses medium risk and invalid authority-bearing input.
Log and event providers use bounded inputs and existing bounded observation projection; permanent follow and arbitrary paths remain absent.
MCP never supplies process cwd as scope; explicit registry aliases resolve and Plugin-cwd omission returns scope_not_resolved.
Closed projected schemas and Kernel validation reject recursively nested force/command/env/cwd authority before journaling or effects.
Host identity and annotations cannot elevate Kernel risk, ownership, scope, or compatibility decisions.
Closing and restarting MCP preserves the detached managed task and reuses its exact run ID with one spawn receipt.
Raw server stdout contains JSON-RPC frames only; foreground stdout/stderr is captured to bounded structuredContent and diagnostics are metadata-only stderr.
MCP lifecycle output preserves shared AgentOperationResult semantics and CLI-visible run/state identity, with only surface provenance differing.
Evidence comes from official Client/StdioClientTransport against the real src/mcp/stdio-server.js process, including list/call/concurrency/close/restart.
